summary: Building a full university portal (similar to University of Africa, Toru-Orua) on the Internet Computer stack with React + Motoko + Internet Identity + Stripe. Includes role-based authentication (Admin, Student, Staff, Parent), public homepage, admissions module, student dashboard, parent portal, admin panel, hostel application, and payment integration.
architectural_notes: Backend: Motoko on Internet Computer (IC) canisters
Payments: Stripe integration (Paystack/Flutterwave not supported on this platform)
Responsive, mobile-friendly UI with clear role-based navigation
Exams & Records is a separate portal/site for results upload, distinct from the main university portal
Protected navigation items must be conditionally rendered based on authentication state — unauthenticated users must never see role-specific menu options
Backend admin role checks must verify the caller's principal against the stored admin profile — mismatches cause false 'Unauthorized' errors even for valid admins

Role-specific login entry points: each portal category (Admin, Staff, Student, Parent) has a dedicated pre-login landing/login page that encodes the intended role — after Internet Identity authentication the user is automatically associated with that role
known_issues: Course addition is broken — users cannot add courses; needs full fix across admin panel and course registration
Adding students, fees, and courses in the admin panel is broken — data does not persist correctly
Role-specific sidebar/menu options are visible to unauthenticated users — they should only appear after login
Profiles are not loading after login — user profile data fails to fetch/display post-authentication for all roles
Admin gets 'Unauthorized — admin only' error when trying to add fees or perform admin actions despite being registered as admin — backend role check is not recognising the principal correctly

existing_features.title: Design and implement a visually compelling public homepage with a deep navy and gold academic theme — clean typography, bold hero section with university name and tagline, navigation bar with links to Student, Staff, Parent, and Partner portals, sections for About/Introduction, Available Programmes, Admission Notices, and Acceptance Process overview, and a footer with contact info and quick links.

Implement role-based authentication using Internet Identity. Support four roles: Admin, Student, Staff, and Parent. After login, detect or prompt the user to select/confirm their role. Store user profiles with role, name, and associated metadata (e.g., student ID for students, staff ID for staff) in the Motoko backend. Route authenticated users to their respective dashboards based on role.

Build an Admissions & Application module. Include a multi-step application form capturing personal info, JAMB registration number, O'Level results (entered as text fields per subject/grade), programme choice, and document upload fields (supporting base64 encoded file references stored in backend). Provide an Admission List Checker page where applicants can enter their JAMB number or name to check admission status.

Create a Student Dashboard accessible only to users with the Student role. Include sections for: Course Registration (list available courses per semester, allow student to register/deregister), Fee Statement (display outstanding and paid fees), Results Viewer (display results per semester with grade and score), and Academic Transcript Download (display a print-ready transcript view).

Create a Parent Dashboard accessible only to users with the Parent role. Allow parents to link their account to a student by entering the student's ID. Once linked, display the linked student's academic results, fee payment status, and course registrations in a read-only view.

Integrate Stripe for online fee payments. On the Student Dashboard fee statement page, add a 'Pay Now' button for outstanding fees. Implement a Stripe Checkout flow. On successful payment, store a payment record (amount, date, reference, fee type) in the Motoko backend and display a receipt. Show full payment history with downloadable receipt views.

Build a Hostel & Accommodation module. Include a public-facing hostel application form (room type preference, session, personal info). Store submitted hostel applications in the backend. Admins can approve or reject hostel applications. Approved students see an acceptance notification on their dashboard.

Build an Admin Panel accessible only to users with the Admin role. Include management sections for: Programmes (add, edit, list), Students (view profiles, update status), Staff (view profiles), Admissions (view applications, approve/reject, post admission decisions), Results (post results per student per course per semester), Fees (define fee items per programme/session), Hostel Applications (approve/reject), and a Messaging tool to send announcements to all students or specific roles.

Create a Staff Dashboard accessible only to users with the Staff role. Display the staff member's profile, assigned courses, and allow staff to view the students enrolled in their courses. Staff can also view announcements sent by Admin.

Fix the Admin Panel so that adding students, fees, and courses fully persists end-to-end. In the Motoko backend, ensure addFeeItem, addCourse, and any student-creation/profile-update functions correctly write to stable state and that the corresponding getters return all stored records reliably. In the frontend, wire the FeeManagementPage, ProgrammeManagementPage, and StudentManagementPage forms to their respective backend mutation hooks so that submitted data is saved to the backend (not just local state) and the lists refresh immediately after submission without a page reload.

Add Nigerian Naira (₦) currency formatting with thousand separators to all fee-related amount fields and displays throughout the application. This includes: the FeeManagementPage fee list and input field, the FeeStatementPage outstanding and paid fee amounts, the PaymentHistoryPage payment amounts, the ReceiptPage payment total, and any other page displaying monetary values. Format numbers as ₦1,000,000.00 using the Nigerian locale (en-NG) or equivalent Intl.NumberFormat formatting.

After login, the navigation sidebar/menu for each role (Student, Staff, Parent, Admin) must display a contextual list of all services and features available to that role. For Students: Course Registration, Fee Statement, Results, Transcript, Hostel Application, Payment History, Announcements. For Staff: My Profile, Assigned Courses, Enrolled Students, Announcements, Exams & Records Portal. For Parents: Linked Student Overview, Student Results, Fee Status, Course Registrations. For Admins: Manage Students, Manage Staff, Manage Courses/Programmes, Manage Fees, Admissions, Results Management, Hostel Applications, Messaging/Announcements, Exams & Records Portal. These service links should be visible in the dashboard sidebar or the role-based dropdown menu in the Navigation component after a user is authenticated.

In the Navigation component, hide all role-specific service menus (Student, Staff, Parent, Admin dropdown/sidebar items) from users who are not authenticated. These menus must only render when the user is logged in via Internet Identity and has a confirmed role. Unauthenticated visitors should only see public links (Home, Admissions, Admission Checker, portal login prompts).

Wrap all role-specific dashboard routes (student/*, staff/*, parent/*, admin/*) and their sidebar navigation links with authentication and role checks. If an unauthenticated user navigates directly to any protected route, redirect them to the homepage or display a login-required message. The RoleGuard component must be applied consistently to every protected page so that the protected content is never rendered without a valid authenticated session and the correct role.

Audit and fix the StudentDashboard, StaffDashboard, AdminDashboard, and ParentDashboard sidebar components so that their service link lists (Course Registration, Fee Statement, Results, etc. for students; Manage Students, Manage Fees, etc. for admins; My Profile, Assigned Courses, etc. for staff; Linked Student Overview, etc. for parents) are conditionally rendered only when the authenticated user's role matches. If the user is unauthenticated or the role does not match, the sidebar must not render the protected service links.

Fix the frontend profile-fetching flow so that after a successful Internet Identity login, the useProfile (or equivalent) React Query hook re-fetches immediately using the authenticated actor. Ensure the actor is fully initialised before the profile query fires, the query key includes the user's principal so it is not stale from a previous session, and any loading/error states are handled gracefully so the UI does not silently display empty profile data.

In the frontend Admin Panel pages (FeeManagementPage, ProgrammeManagementPage, StudentManagementPage, ResultsManagementPage, MessagingPage, HostelManagementPage), surface a clear, human-readable error toast or inline message when a backend mutation returns an authorization error, so admins know immediately if the session/actor has expired and they need to re-authenticate, rather than silently failing or showing a raw error string.

In the frontend, ensure the authenticated actor passed to all admin mutation hooks (addFeeItem, addCourse, registerStudent, postResult, sendAnnouncement, approveHostelApplication, rejectHostelApplication, updateAdmissionStatus, etc.) is the fully authenticated actor created after Internet Identity login — not the anonymous actor. The useActor hook must supply the identity-authenticated actor to every mutation on admin pages, and the actor must be re-initialised if the identity changes.

Ensure every admin panel page (FeeManagementPage, ProgrammeManagementPage, StudentManagementPage, ResultsManagementPage, MessagingPage, HostelManagementPage, AdmissionsManagementPage, StaffManagementPage) is wrapped with the RoleGuard component requiring the 'admin' role, and that the RoleGuard reads the profile from the backend using the authenticated principal — not from stale local state or cache from a previous session.

After a successful admin action (add fee, add course, register student, post result, approve/reject admission, approve/reject hostel application, send announcement), display a clear success toast notification and immediately invalidate and refetch the relevant React Query list queries so the updated data appears in the UI without a full page reload.

In the frontend, add a visible 'Claim Admin Account' button or setup prompt on the Admin Dashboard (or a dedicated setup page at /admin/setup) that is shown only when no admin profile exists yet. When clicked, it calls the bootstrapAdmin backend function using the fully authenticated actor (not the anonymous actor) to register the currently logged-in Internet Identity principal as the admin. On success, display a clear confirmation toast ('Admin account successfully created') and redirect to the Admin Dashboard.

On the Admin Dashboard homepage and the admin setup page, display the currently authenticated user's real Internet Identity principal and their stored profile name prominently in the admin profile card, so the admin can confirm their account identity at a glance. Label it clearly as 'Your Admin Principal' alongside the display name stored in their profile.

Create a Portal Selection page at /portal (or as a prominent section on the homepage) that displays four distinct portal entry cards: Student Portal, Staff Portal, Admin Portal, and Parent Portal. Each card must have a clear label, a brief description of the portal, and a 'Login' or 'Access Portal' button. Clicking a card navigates the visitor to that role's dedicated login page.

Create four dedicated role-specific login pages — /login/student, /login/staff, /login/admin, and /login/parent — each branded with the role name and a short description (e.g., 'Student Portal — Access your courses, results, and fee statements'). Each page must show an 'Login with Internet Identity' button that triggers the Internet Identity login flow. After successful login, if the user already has a profile with the matching role, redirect them directly to their role dashboard. If the user has no profile yet, pre-select the matching role in the ProfileSetupModal (so the role dropdown defaults to the role corresponding to the login page they used) before prompting them to complete profile setup. This eliminates the need for the user to manually choose their role after logging in via a role-specific portal page.

Update the public homepage and the Navigation component so that the portal links (Student Portal, Staff Portal, Admin Portal, Parent Portal) that were previously pointing directly to dashboard routes now point to the corresponding role-specific login pages (/login/student, /login/staff, /login/admin, /login/parent) for unauthenticated visitors. For already-authenticated users with a confirmed role, these links should still navigate directly to their respective dashboards.

new_feature_requests.summary: Role-based authentication: Admin, Student, Staff, Parent accounts via Internet Identity
Public homepage with university intro, program pages, admission notices, and portal links
Admissions & application module with forms, document upload, and admission list checker
Student dashboard with course registration, fee statements, and results viewer/transcript download
Parent portal to track student progress
Admin panel to manage programmes, fees, students, staff, results, approvals, and messaging
Hostel/accommodation application forms and acceptance notifications
Online payment integration using Stripe for fee payments with records and receipts
Fix course addition functionality — make course management fully functional across all relevant modules
Build a separate Exams and Records site/portal for student results upload and management

Fix the course addition and management functionality end-to-end. In the Motoko backend, ensure the addCourse function correctly persists courses and that getCourses returns all stored courses reliably. Verify that the CourseRegistration, ResultsManagement, and ProgrammeManagement pages all read from and write to the same shared course store. Ensure the admin can add a course (with code, name, semester, programme) and it immediately appears in the student course registration list and the results posting form without requiring a page reload.

Fix course registration persistence for students. Currently, course registration uses local toggle state. Replace it with backend-persisted registration: add registerCourse and deregisterCourse backend functions that store a student's registered courses keyed by their principal. Expose useRegisterCourse and useDeregisterCourse mutation hooks and a useGetStudentCourses query hook in useQueries.ts. Update CourseRegistrationPage to show the student's actual registered courses fetched from the backend, and update the StudentDashboard credit summary to reflect persisted registered course count.

Fix the admin Results Management page so that result posting is fully functional. Ensure the student ID field accepts valid student IDs, the course dropdown is populated from the live course list, and the addResult backend function stores results correctly. Add a useGetAllStudents query to populate a student selector dropdown (listing all users with the student role by name and ID) so admins do not need to type IDs manually. After a result is posted, display a success confirmation and allow the admin to post another result.

Fix the admin Messaging page so that announcements are stored in the Motoko backend and are retrievable. Add a sendAnnouncement backend function that stores a message with its target role, content, sender principal, and timestamp. Add a getAnnouncements backend function that returns messages filtered by role. Update the MessagingPage to persist announcements via the backend. Update the StaffDashboard to fetch and display announcements targeted to 'staff' or 'all'. Update the StudentDashboard to fetch and display announcements targeted to 'students' or 'all'.

Build a separate Exams and Records portal as a distinct frontend application within the same project. This portal is called 'Exams and Records' and is accessible at a dedicated route prefix (e.g., /exams-records). It has its own login via Internet Identity restricted to users with the 'staff' or 'admin' role. The portal includes: (1) A Results Upload page where authorised staff/admin can upload student results by selecting a student, course, semester, entering a score and grade, and submitting — stored in the same backend results store. (2) A Results Browser page to search and view all uploaded results filtered by student ID, course, or semester. (3) A Bulk Results Entry view where staff can enter results for multiple students in a course/semester in a single table form. (4) A dashboard home showing summary stats: total results uploaded, number of students with results, number of courses with results. The portal uses the same deep navy and gold academic theme as the main site.
Fix adding students, fees, and courses so they fully persist and work end-to-end in the admin panel
Add Nigerian Naira currency formatting with thousand separators on all fee-related fields and displays
After login, the sidebar/menu for student, staff, and other roles should display a list of available services/features applicable to that role

Fix auth-gating: role-specific sidebar/menu options (admin, student, staff, parent) must only be visible and accessible after the user is logged in; unauthenticated users should not see protected navigation items
Fix profiles not loading after login — user profiles fail to display/fetch after successful authentication across all roles

Fix the backend getProfile (or equivalent) function so that it reliably returns the stored user profile for the calling principal immediately after login. Ensure the profile lookup is keyed correctly by principal and that the stable variable holding profiles is read and written consistently — no silent failures or empty optional returns when a profile exists.

Fix 'Unauthorized — admin only' error when admin tries to add fees or perform admin actions despite being registered as admin. The backend admin role check is not recognising the authenticated principal as admin.

Fix the backend admin role check so that when a user registered with the 'admin' role calls privileged functions (such as addFeeItem, addCourse, registerStudent, updateAdmissionStatus, postResult, sendAnnouncement, approveHostelApplication, etc.), the caller's principal is correctly matched against the stored admin profile. The role check must read from the same stable profiles store used during registration and must not return #err("Unauthorized — admin only") for a principal that has a stored profile with role = #admin.

Audit all admin-gated backend functions in main.mo and consolidate the admin authorization check into a single shared helper (e.g., isAdmin(caller: Principal): Bool) that reads from the stable profiles store. Replace any ad-hoc or duplicated role checks throughout the file with calls to this helper to ensure consistency.

Audit every admin-gated function in backend/main.mo (addFeeItem, addCourse, registerStudent, updateAdmissionStatus, postResult, sendAnnouncement, approveHostelApplication, rejectHostelApplication, updateStudentStatus, and all other privileged operations) and replace all ad-hoc role checks with a single shared isAdmin(caller: Principal): Bool helper that reads exclusively from the same stable profiles HashMap/TrieMap used during profile registration. The helper must return true if and only if a stored profile exists for that principal with role = #admin. Remove any hardcoded principal whitelists, environment-variable checks, or duplicate inline role checks that could diverge from this store.

Fix the profile registration flow in backend/main.mo so that when saveCallerUserProfile (or equivalent) is called with role = #admin, it writes the profile to the stable store under the exact caller principal that will be passed to all subsequent mutation calls. Ensure there is no race condition, key mismatch, or silent overwrite that causes the stored role to differ from what was submitted.
Provide real/persistent admin account credentials or setup instructions so the user can use a genuine admin account rather than test/placeholder details

In the Motoko backend, implement a bootstrapAdmin function (or equivalent initialisation mechanism) that allows the first authenticated caller to claim the admin role permanently, storing their principal in the stable profiles store with role = #admin. This ensures the admin account is a real, persistent account tied to the user's actual Internet Identity principal — not a placeholder or test account. The function should only work once (or only if no admin profile yet exists) to prevent takeover.

Add role-selection landing pages: when a visitor clicks a portal category (Admin, Staff, Student, Parent), route them to a dedicated login page for that role so that after Internet Identity login they are automatically registered/recognised under the selected role without needing to manually choose their role post-login
